name: Fm Bank and Trust
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank with 14 locations across Arkansas and Missouri, offering personal, business, and agricultural banking services with digital and mobile options.
description_long: FM Bank and Trust is a regional community bank operating across Arkansas and Missouri with fourteen physical branch locations. The bank has been serving local communities with a focus on relationship-based banking, positioning itself as a financial partner that understands and supports local markets, particularly in agricultural communities. The bank is FDIC-insured and regulated under NMLS #440455. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking products. For personal customers, FM Bank and Trust provides checking accounts with debit cards, online and mobile banking, direct deposit, and bill pay functionality. Business customers can access business checking accounts with cash management services, digital banking, and personalized service from local bankers. The bank has a dedicated agricultural lending division that serves farming families with specialized loan products. Digital services include online loan applications, mobile check deposit, digital wallets for smartphone payments, account transfers, and 24/7 online banking access through both retail and business portals. FM Bank and Trust differentiates itself through strong agricultural lending expertise and a commitment to community engagement. The bank explicitly markets itself as a partner for farming operations and emphasizes relationship banking over transactional services. Their promise centers on personalized service, convenient branch access across two states, and understanding local market needs. The bank offers employee payroll services and bill pay through eCorp Business Online Banking for business customers. As a regional community bank, FM Bank and Trust serves a specific geographic market and is best suited for customers in Arkansas and Missouri seeking local banking relationships. The bank competes on service quality and community focus rather than on product innovation or competitive rates. Customers should verify specific account terms, fee structures, and interest rates through direct contact, as the website emphasizes service philosophy rather than detailed product specifications.
services: Personal checking accounts with debit cards
Business checking accounts with cash management services
Online and mobile banking access (retail and business)
Direct deposit for personal and business accounts
Online bill pay and account transfers
Agricultural lending and farm business loans
Digital wallet integration for smartphones and smartwatches
Debit card fraud alerts and lost card replacement
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
14 convenient branch locations across Arkansas and Missouri
Specialized agricultural lending division with dedicated professionals
Online loan applications with digital approval process
Mobile check deposit using smartphone camera
Business payroll direct deposit and cash management services
Digital wallet support for contactless smartphone payments
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only serves Arkansas and Missouri customers
No mention of competitive interest rates or fee structures on website
Small bank may have fewer product options compared to national banks
Website does not specify minimum account balances or opening requirements
Limited information about agricultural loan terms and eligibility criteria
best_for: Agricultural business owners and farming families in Arkansas and Missouri
Small business owners seeking local banking relationships and personalized service
Customers in FM Bank and Trust's service areas preferring community banking over national banks
Businesses needing payroll processing and cash management services
diagnosis: FM Bank and Trust is best for agricultural businesses, small business owners, and individuals in Arkansas and Missouri seeking community-based banking with personalized service and local market expertise. The main caveat is that this is a regional bank with limited geographic reach; customers outside Arkansas and Missouri cannot open accounts, and those within the service area should compare specific product terms and fees directly with the bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured community bank with 14 locations across Arkansas and Missouri offering personal, business, and agricultural banking services.
